user_query: Summarize INC3190020
route_to: ServiceNow (fetch incident) + Knowledge Base (STTM for context)
expected_filter: SN: number=INC3190020 | KB: Core_Banking_Raw_INT_CUR_STTM.xlsx Segment Register + Raw_to_INT tab filtered on Segment='KYC Profile' AND PII Sensitive='Y'
expected_incidents_text: INC3190020 + KB links to Core Banking STTM
expected_summary: Incident Summary: INC3190020 (Resolved, Critical) - Core Banking KYC Profile segment had 8 PII-sensitive fields (national_id, passport_number, tax_id, etc.) loaded unencrypted into int_core_banking.kyc_profile. A pipeline config change on PL-CB-11-KYC-PROFILE-RAW-INT disabled the encryption data flow. Encryption restored, cleartext data purged. Awaiting security sign-off. Assigned to: Dana Sample (D0004) Opened: 2026-05-28 | Resolved: 2026-05-29 Supporting KB Links: 1. Core_Banking_Raw_INT_CUR_STTM.xlsx > Segment Register - confirms KYC Profile has 8 PII-sensitive fields 2. Core_Banking_Raw_INT_CUR_STTM.xlsx > Raw_to_INT tab (rows RI-00xxx for KYC Profile) - lists each field with PII Sensitive=Y flag 3. Core_Banking_Raw_INT_CUR_STTM.xlsx > File Details - source file: kyc_profile_extract.dat, copybook: CBK_KYC_PROF
notes: Strong KB tie: the STTM PII field inventory directly corroborates the incident (8 sensitive fields). Agent should surface which specific fields were exposed by cross-referencing the STTM.

expected_summary: Incident Summary: INC3205060 (New, Critical) - Real Estate Pricing Property Valuation Master AVM vendor API returning HTTP 503 for 6+ hours. No data landed at /mnt/landing/rel/vendor/valuation/. Tables raw_rel.valuation, int_rel.property_valuation_master, and cur_rel.property_valuation_master are stale. Impacting downstream pricing benchmarks and Comparable Sales Master pipeline. Currently unassigned. Opened: 2026-06-03 Supporting KB Links: 1. Real_Estate_Pricing_STTM_Raw_INT_CUR.xlsx > File Details - Source: valuation_master.dat, Type: Vendor API extract, Format: Parquet, Frequency: Daily, Sensitive: Yes 2. Real_Estate_Pricing_STTM_Raw_INT_CUR.xlsx > Segment Register - raw_rel.valuation -> int_rel.property_valuation_master -> cur_rel.property_valuation_master, 25 fields, PK: valuation_id 3. Real_Estate_Pricing_Requirements.xlsx - business justification: supports baseline market value and price benchmarking

user_query: Tell me about INC3025540 and any supporting docs
route_to: ServiceNow (fetch incident) + Knowledge Base (STTM for context)
expected_filter: SN: number=INC3025540 | KB: Debit_Card_Raw_INT_CUR_STTM_Merged.xlsx Segment Register + Raw_to_INT/INT_to_CUR for Transaction Clearing
expected_incidents_text: INC3025540 + KB links to Debit Card STTM
expected_summary: Incident Summary: INC3025540 (Closed, High) - 14,200 duplicate clearing_txn_id values in cur_txn_clearing after a partial pipeline retry. INT-to-CUR pipeline lacked idempotency guard. Fixed by adding MERGE INTO logic keyed on clearing_txn_id. Deduplicated and pipeline updated. Assigned to: Alex Vega (T0002) | Closed: 2026-05-06 Supporting KB Links: 1. Debit_Card_Raw_INT_CUR_STTM_Merged.xlsx > Segment Register - Transaction Clearing PK = clearing_txn_id, 100 fields, INT: int_txn_clearing, CUR: cur_txn_clearing 2. Debit_Card_Raw_INT_CUR_STTM_Merged.xlsx > INT_to_CUR tab - transformation rules for Transaction Clearing showing how clearing_txn_id should be used as merge key 3. Debit_Card_CUR_to_ASL_STTM.xlsx - downstream ASL mapping from cur_txn_clearing (for assessing blast radius of duplicates)
notes: Closed incident. KB confirms clearing_txn_id as the PK and provides the MERGE logic that was missing. Also links to CUR-to-ASL STTM for downstream impact assessment.

user_query: Summarize incident INC3225110 for me
route_to: ServiceNow (fetch incident) + Knowledge Base (STTM for context)
expected_filter: SN: number=INC3225110 | KB: logistics_enterprise_sttm_workbook.xlsx Source_Catalog + Hop1_Landing_Raw for shipment_line_src
expected_incidents_text: INC3225110 + KB links to Logistics STTM
expected_summary: Incident Summary: INC3225110 (In Progress, High) - Logistics shipment_line_src records not parsing into raw.logistics_shipment_line. Source switched from comma to pipe delimiter without notice. All 13 mapped columns returning null in RAW. INT and Curated layers have zero new shipment line records. Shipment headers (shipment_src) unaffected. Assigned to: Alex Vega (T0002) Opened: 2026-06-02 Supporting KB Links: 1. logistics_enterprise_sttm_workbook.xlsx > Source_Catalog - shipment_line_src: API extract, JSON format, Daily, PK: shipment_line_id 2. logistics_enterprise_sttm_workbook.xlsx > Hop1_Landing_Raw - 13 field mappings for shipment_line_src (LOG-H1-0038 onwards): trim() and cast() transformations that depend on correct delimiter parsing 3. logistics_enterprise_sttm_workbook.xlsx > DQ_Rules - Not Null checks on shipment_line_id and shipment_id that should have flagged the null records earlier
notes: Open incident. KB provides the expected source format (JSON) and all 13 field mappings. Agent should highlight that the STTM Hop1 transformations assume correct delimiter, which is the root cause.

Note: 2 other open Core Banking incidents exist (INC3185010 - row count mismatch, INC3190020 - PII config error) but these are data quality issues, not pipeline failures. 1 closed pipeline incident: INC3011201 - Transaction Ledger pipeline timeout (resolved).

Core Banking has 3 open incidents but only 1 is a pipeline failure (configuration_item LIKE "PL-%" AND description indicates pipeline broke). INC3190020 also has a PL-* config_item but is a PII config error, not a pipeline failure - agent must distinguish using description/cause context. Agent should only return INC3235130, not INC3185010 or INC3190020.

user_query: What were the resolution notes for incidents similar to INC3235130?
expected_intent: Find Historical Resolution for Similar Incidents
route_to: ServiceNow (fetch INC) -> ServiceNow (search closed incidents: same datasource + same cause)
data_source: Core Banking - Deposit Account Master (open) -> Core Banking - Transaction Ledger (historical match)
expected_filter: Step 1: Fetch INC3235130 -> Core Banking, cause='Authentication issue' Step 2: Search closed incidents WHERE (short_description LIKE '%Core Banking%' OR description LIKE '%Core Banking%') AND (cause LIKE '%authentication%' OR cause LIKE '%timeout%') AND state=Closed
expected_summary: Current incident: INC3235130 (In Progress, Critical) - PL-CB-02-DEPOSIT-ACCT-MASTER-RAW-INGEST failed, auth token expired. Similar historical incident found: INC3011201 (Closed, Critical) - PL-CB-04-TRANSACTION-LEDGER-DAILY-INGEST failed, copy activity timeout. Similarity: Same datasource (Core Banking) + Same cause type (pipeline failure / source connectivity) Resolution notes from INC3011201: "ADF copy activity timed out because the Core Banking source system was slow. Re-triggered PL-CB-04-TRANSACTION-LEDGER-DAILY-INGEST after source recovered. Data landed in int_core_banking.transaction_ledger, CUR layer refreshed, and row counts reconciled across RAW, INT, and CUR." Suggested action: Similar ADF pipeline recovery pattern - rotate/renew auth token, re-trigger PL-CB-02 pipeline, validate row counts across layers.
notes: Strong match: same datasource (Core Banking) + same cause type (pipeline failure). Agent must search by datasource in short_description/description (not config_item). Agent should extract the resolution pattern and suggest analogous steps for the current incident.

user_query: What was done to resolve incidents like INC3198040?
expected_intent: Find Historical Resolution for Similar Incidents
route_to: ServiceNow (fetch INC) -> ServiceNow (search closed incidents: same cause type)
data_source: Debit Card - Fraud Alerts (open) -> Debit Card - POS Terminal Master (historical match)
expected_filter: Step 1: Fetch INC3198040 -> Debit Card, cause='Vendor outage' Step 2: Search closed incidents WHERE cause LIKE '%vendor%outage%' OR cause LIKE '%vendor%maintenance%' AND state=Closed
expected_summary: Current incident: INC3198040 (On Hold, High) - Fraud Alerts feed stale for 2 days, vendor outage. Similar historical incident found: INC3032200 (Closed, Moderate) - Debit Card POS Terminal Master feed delayed 2 days due to vendor maintenance. Similarity: Same datasource (Debit Card) + Same cause (vendor outage / feed delayed 2 days) Resolution notes from INC3032200: "Vendor outage caused 2-day feed gap. Resolution steps: (1) Confirmed with vendor that maintenance was complete. (2) Vendor re-delivered pos_terminal_master.dat for both missed days. (3) Re-triggered PL-DC-11-POS-TERMINAL-RAW-INGEST for backfill. (4) Validated row counts across RAW, INT, CUR. Terminal_id lookups reconciled. (5) Added vendor maintenance schedule to DEPS monitoring calendar. (6) Configured file-arrival SLA alert at 24-hour threshold so delayed feeds are flagged before downstream pipelines run on stale data." Suggested action: Follow same playbook - confirm vendor recovery, request backfill of missed fraud_alerts.dat files, re-trigger pipeline, validate, and add SLA alert for this feed.
notes: Strong match: same datasource (Debit Card) + identical cause (vendor outage, 2-day gap). Historical resolution provides a step-by-step playbook directly applicable to the current incident.

user_query: Can you find past resolution notes for something similar to INC3215090?
expected_intent: Find Historical Resolution for Similar Incidents
route_to: ServiceNow (fetch INC) -> ServiceNow (search closed incidents: same cause + same datasource)
data_source: Customer Enterprise - Interaction (open) -> Customer Enterprise - Interaction (historical match)
expected_filter: Step 1: Fetch INC3215090 -> Customer Enterprise, cause='Kafka consumer lag' Step 2: Search closed incidents WHERE cause LIKE '%Kafka%' OR cause LIKE '%consumer%lag%' AND state=Closed
expected_summary: Current incident: INC3215090 (New, High) - raw.customer_cust_interaction lagging 4 hours, interaction_id consumer offset growing. Unassigned. Similar historical incident found: INC2920400 (Closed, High) - Same feed (customer_interaction_src) experienced 6-hour Kafka consumer lag after broker restart. Similarity: Same datasource (Customer Enterprise) + Same cause (Kafka consumer lag) + Same feed (customer_interaction_src) Resolution notes from INC2920400: "Kafka consumer group rebalancing storm after broker restart. Resolution steps: (1) Increased session.timeout.ms from 10s to 45s to prevent premature partition revocation. (2) Increased max.poll.interval.ms from 300s to 600s to allow longer processing windows. (3) Set group.instance.id for static membership to avoid unnecessary rebalances on transient disconnects. (4) Consumer backlog cleared within 2 hours of config push. All interaction events caught up and INT/Curated layers refreshed. (5) Added consumer lag alerting at 2-hour threshold on the interaction_id topic. (6) Documented Kafka tuning parameters in the platform runbook." Suggested action: Check if Kafka tuning from INC2920400 is still in place. If lag persists despite those settings, investigate broker health or upstream volume spike. The 2-hour lag alert added previously should have fired - verify alerting config.
notes: Strongest possible match: same datasource + same cause + same feed (customer_interaction_src). Historical resolution provides exact Kafka tuning parameters. Agent should note that prior fixes should already be in place and suggest investigating why they did not prevent recurrence.

Opened: 2026-05-18 | Closed: 2026-05-20 Total: 1 incident.
notes: Direct match on cause field. User's search term 'source data defects' matches cause='Source data defect'. The cause field now uses short keywords, making LIKE pattern matching straightforward.
